conditions.name: Gluten Intolerance
conditions.summary: Also known as non-celiac gluten sensitivity, so the symptoms you experience do not cause the same type of damage to the small intestine as does celiac disease.
foods_to_avoid: Processed foods (Breads, Cereals, Pastas, Cakes, Pies, and etc.)

conditions.name: Lactose Intolerance
conditions.summary: A condition that makes it hard to digest the sugar in milk and milk products, called lactose. The condition is usually harmless, but its symptomps can cause discomfort.

conditions.name: Fructose Intolerance
conditions.summary: A condition that makes it difficult to digest fructose, which is a sugar found naturally in fruits, fruit juices, some vegetables, and honey.
foods_to_avoid: Fruits, such as: apples, pears, watermelon
Vegetables, such as: asparagus, artichokes, onions, and garlic
substitutes: Fruits: bananas, oranges, cantaloupe, and berries
Vegetables: beans, carrots, lettuce, spinach, and tomatoes
Grains: plain rice, oats, white bread, and pasta

conditions.name: Egg Sensitivity
conditions.summary: A non-allergic adverse reaction to eggs or egg proteins that causes digestive and skin-related symptoms.
foods_to_avoid: Pasta and noodles (if made with egg)
substitutes: Flax egg (flaxseed meal mixed with water) for baking
Chia egg (chia seeds mixed with water) for baking
Applesauce or mashed banana for baking
Chickpea water as an egg white substitute

conditions.name: Food Color Sensitivity
conditions.summary: A non-allergic adverse reaction to synthetic food colors that cause digestive and behavioral symptoms. Commonly reactive dyes include Red 40, Yellow 5, Yellow 6, Blue 1, Blue 2, and Red 3.
substitutes: Red/Pink: Beet juice, strawberries, or pomegranate
Orange: turmeric, carrot juice, or sweet potato
